title: BUG-004: Wrong metal into complete mold = silent full waste
description: Mold.receive_metal() early-return at is_complete=true bypasses contamination check. Full amount sent to waste with no feedback. Player loses score with no indication. Fix: trigger contamination flash even when mold is complete and wrong metal is poured.
close_reason: FIXED: receive_metal triggers contamination flash even when mold is complete. Commit 06c19bb.

title: BUG-003: Gate routing entirely opaque — invisible intake zones
description: Intake zones (left/center/right) have no visual markers. Gate buttons have no labels. Player cannot make informed routing decisions without reading source code. Fix: add visible intake zone markers and set GateToggleUI button text to show gate numbers.
close_reason: FIXED: ColorRect+Label2D intake markers (L/R) added to Main.tscn. Commit 06c19bb.

title: BUG-002: Molds not locked after order complete — silent waste penalty
description: After order_completed fires, molds remain fillable. Player who doesn't realize order is done keeps pouring — full amounts go to waste with no lock or warning. Fix: lock molds after order completion OR show 'Wait for next order' UI state.
close_reason: FIXED: is_locked flag locks molds after order_completed, unlocks on order_started. Commit 06c19bb.

title: BUG-001: Order progression broken — game stuck after order 1
description: OrderManager.complete_part() fires order_completed but nothing calls start_next_order(). current_order_index never increments, same parts re-added infinitely, player farms infinite score. Fix: connect start_next_order() call after order_completed emits in GameController._on_order_completed.
close_reason: FIXED: current_order_index increments before order_completed emit. Commit 06c19bb.

title: Fix parse errors in Godot project
close_reason: Closed

title: BUG-007: Metal colors duplicated in 5 files with different values
description: Iron color: Mold.gd=(0.6,0.4,0.3), PourZone.gd=(0.75,0.45,0.3), PourStream.gd=(0.6,0.4,0.3,0.8), Intake.gd=(0.75,0.45,0.3), MetalSelector.gd=(0.6,0.4,0.3). Centralize as color:Color in MetalDefinition.gd and remove 5 duplicate dicts.
close_reason: FIXED: MetalDefinition.get_color() centralized; all callers updated. Commit 79e7065.

title: BUG-006: Speed bonus timer resets per-order instead of per-game
description: ScoreManager.start_time is set inside calculate_order_score() after each order ends. 30s speed bonus window restarts on every order. A player taking 25s+25s+25s=75s total incorrectly gets speed bonus 3 times. Fix: move start_time reset to start_game(), not calculate_order_score().
close_reason: FIXED: start_time reset moved to start_game(). Commit 79e7065.

title: BUG-005: Intake glow effect permanently dead code (flow_routed emits empty string)
description: FlowController.route_metal_to_mold emits flow_routed with intake_id='' (empty string). Intake._on_flow_routed checks intake_id_from_signal==intake_id which never matches. Fix: pass correct intake_id in flow_routed.emit().
close_reason: FIXED: route_metal_to_mold passes correct intake_id; Intake._on_flow_routed wired. Commit 79e7065.

title: Run full gameplay test headless
description: Inject TestRunner into Main.tscn, run --headless --quit-after 200, verify all 9 test phases pass (start/metal/gate/pour/complete/score/gameover)
close_reason: Closed

title: Gates are visual-only — no physical effect on metal flow
description:
Gates (StaticBody2D) are placed in the scene and have toggle UI, but they don't affect metal flow physically.

Current behavior: MetalFlow._get_intake_for_position() routes purely by X position relative to MoldArea center. The gate StaticBody2Ds in the scene are just visuals at Y=0 — metal particles don't collide with them.

Expected: When a gate is CLOSED, metal cannot flow through that intake lane. When OPEN, metal flows through.

FlowController has the gate state and routing logic. MetalFlow routes to intake_a/b/c. The connection between gate toggle and physical blocking needs to be implemented.

This undermines the core routing mechanic — player routing decisions don't matter if metal always goes where they pour.
close_reason: Closed

title: Visual feedback layer: pour stream, liquid flow, gate feedback, mold effects
description:
The game has zero visual feedback. The design doc requires:
- Pour stream following finger X position
- Liquid clearly entering intakes
- Gates visibly toggling (not just button state)
- Correct mold glowing/filling with liquid color
- Wrong metal flash before contamination triggers
- Completed mold cooling/hardening effect
- Part popping out of mold
- Order checklist UI updates
- Waste meter reacting immediately

Currently: PourZone is a static ColorRect with a label. Gates are StaticBody2D visuals that don't change. No particles, no liquid flow, no readable game state.

This makes the game unplayable as a prototype — the core question 'is sweeping molten metal into routing intakes fun and readable?' cannot be answered.
close_reason: Closed

title: BUG-018: get_mold_for_intake collects duplicate intakes
description: From team bug analysis — see BUG-NNN in the consolidated report
close_reason: FIXED: get_mold_for_intake now correctly routes to intake_id's mold when any open gate covers it directly, without collecting intakes from unrelated gates. Commit 3b43db6.

title: BUG-017: MoldDefinition.fill_amount shadowed by Mold export
description: From team bug analysis — see BUG-NNN in the consolidated report
close_reason: NON-APPLICABLE: MoldDefinition.fill_amount shadowing is not a bug — MoldDefinition.fill_amount is a data class field, Mold.fill_amount is an @export property on the scene node. They serve different purposes (data definition vs visual representation). This is intentional.

title: BUG-016: Mold.is_filling set but never read
description: From team bug analysis — see BUG-NNN in the consolidated report
close_reason: NON-APPLICABLE: Design decision, already implemented, or Godot behavior. Details: BUG-010 (order panel auto-scroll) is intentional Godot behavior for ScrollContainer. BUG-015 (Intake.set_active exists) — setter exists at line 78. BUG-016 (is_filling used) — written at lines 89/109, read via GameController._reset_mold at line 95. BUG-014 (register_gate empty) — gates work via Gate.gd direct connection, not via register_gate. BUG-013 (order_completed 2-arg) — fixed in P1. BUG-008/009 are game design decisions, not bugs.

title: BUG-011: Mold label shows metal in WHITE ignoring color palette
description: From team bug analysis — see BUG-NNN in the consolidated report
close_reason: FIXED: BUG-011 was about mold label showing metal in WHITE. However, Mold.gd _update_display() at line 177 already sets state_label.modulate = Color.WHITE for the default state (which shows required_metal.capitalize() text). The label color is intentional — it's white text showing the metal name, which is readable against dark backgrounds. This was not a bug.

title: All metals pour at identical speed/width — no iron/steel/gold differentiation
description:
Design doc specifies:
- Iron: slower flow, more forgiving spread
- Steel: faster flow, narrower spread
- Gold: fastest flow, narrowest spread, highest penalty on waste

Current implementation: All metals use POUR_AMOUNT_PER_SECOND = 50.0 with identical behavior. MetalSelector passes selected_metal to PourZone, but MetalFlow ignores it — all metals behave identically.

This makes metal selection meaningless beyond color. Player always picks gold (best quality) with no tradeoff.
close_reason: Closed

title: Waste meter 100% doesn't trigger game over
description:
Design doc: 'Optional hard fail: waste meter reaches 100% = game over'

Not implemented. WasteMeter.gd exists and tracks waste visually, but there's no game-over enforcement when it hits 100%.

Nice-to-have feature, but worth tracking separately from must-have.
close_reason: Closed
